behavior_summary: U8P_DOOREGG implements egg lifecycle handlers. The primary behavior on area-exit (U8P_DOOREGG::leaveFastArea) normalizes an internal egg id by subtracting 0x64 when the id is at least 0x64.

The U8P_DOOREGG::hatch process advances or alters the egg's id/state (adds 0x64 in typical flow) and triggers world-side effects: it scans item/object slots for items whose Item::getQuality matches a snapshot of the egg id and then spawns activations on linked objects (doors/sliders).

There is a special-case when the egg id equals 0xC8 (200): a matching item with low-byte quality 0xFA causes a DOOR_NS::useDoor spawn, a suspend/wait, and then the item's quality is updated with an OR 0x0400 flag.
notable_control_flow: U8P_DOOREGG::leaveFastArea: read Egg::getEggId(this); if >= 0x64 write Egg::setEggId(getEggId(this) - 0x64). No other side effects observed.

U8P_DOOREGG::hatch: branch on Egg::getEggId(this) == 0xC8 (special door/quality flag path) else snapshot egg id into local5, write Egg::setEggId(local5 + 0x64) then run repeated per-slot/per-type scan blocks comparing Item::getQuality(...) == local5 and spawning SLIDER or DOOR actions.
Spawns are used to invoke DOOR_NS::useDoor or SLIDER::activateSlider / SLIDER::deactivateSlider. At least one DOOR_NS::useDoor is followed by suspend (synchronous wait) before modifying item quality.
open_questions: What exact in-game items/slots correspond to the repeated scan blocks in U8P_DOOREGG::hatch (which item groups/indices are iterated)?
What semantic meaning do the numeric id increments/decrements of 0x64 represent in gameplay terms (stages, tiers, or encoding of sub-state)?
What does the quality low-byte value 0xFA signify and what gameplay event is encoded by setting the 0x0400 bit on an item's quality?
Are there any global flags or external state changes outside item quality and egg id that this code relies on but which are not present in the decompiled fragments?

main_function.name: U8P_DOOREGG::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: Area-exit handler that normalizes / reduces an egg's internal id by 0x64 when the egg id is at least 0x64, likely to roll back or change egg state when leaving a trigger region.
entry_conditions: Triggered when an actor leaves the fast-area trigger region (U8P_DOOREGG::leaveFastArea event).
Acts only when Egg::getEggId(this) >= 0x64; otherwise it is a no-op.

function_map.name: U8P_DOOREGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: area-exit state normalizer for egg id

function_map.name: U8P_DOOREGG::hatch
function_map.role: egg-processing / hatching routine that advances egg id and triggers door/slider activations and item quality modifications

function_map.name: Item::getQuality
function_map.role: read item quality/metadata for matching and flag checks

function_map.name: Item::setQuality
function_map.role: write modified quality/metadata (e.g., OR in flags)

function_map.name: spawn
function_map.role: asynchronously start a method/process on a target object

function_map.name: suspend
function_map.role: suspend current process (used after spawning when waiting for result)

external_calls.purpose: Read the egg's id to decide whether to subtract 0x64.
Write back egg id = oldId - 0x64 when condition met.
Read current egg id to branch on special-case 0xC8 and to snapshot into local5 for item comparisons.
Advance egg id by writing local5 + 0x64 in the non-0xC8 flow.
Compare item quality low-byte or full quality against egg id snapshot (local5) or test for 0xFA low-byte in special branch.
Set or OR-in quality flags (e.g., OR 0x0400) on items in the 0xC8 branch.
Invoke DOOR_NS::useDoor or SLIDER::<activate|deactivate> on target objects asynchronously as part of world-side activation.
Trigger door usage/action on matched item/object; in at least one branch the process suspends afterwards.
Activate slider mechanisms associated with items whose quality equals the egg snapshot.
Deactivate slider mechanisms in alternate repeated scan blocks for matching items.
Wait for a spawned DOOR_NS::useDoor to complete in the special-case branch before continuing to modify item quality.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_DOOREGG implements egg lifecycle handlers. The primary behavior on area-exit (U8P_DOOREGG::leaveFastArea) normalizes an internal egg id by subtracting 0x64 when the id is at least 0x64.
